Abstract#1: A faxed copy of page #2 from Kodak publication No. AS-57. The page comes from a catalog meant to record the types of aerial films offered by Kodak, as well as the price per roll of each film. Film types include Kodak Double-X Aerographic film, Kodak Infrared Aerographic film, Kodak Panatomic-X Aerographic II film, and Kodak Plus-X Aerographic film. All films come with an ESTAR base.

Collection Name: Panoramic Photography from Fire Towers on Colorado National Lands
Content Note: In the interest of preserving and presenting the historical record, the University Libraries collects materials that document and demonstrate the perspectives presented by the creator. As a result, this item contains outdated language that does not reflect the current preferred terminology, or the values and norms of the University Libraries. On December 2021, the U.S. Board on Geographic Names officially renamed Colorado's Squaw Mountain to Mestaa'ėhehe Mountain.
Title#1: Film exposure record for a photograph depicting Roosevelt National Forest
Abstract#1: A film exposure record noting the location and photographic details of a panoramic photograph captured from a fire tower at Thorodin Mountain in Colorado. The photograph captures key areas throughout Roosevelt and Arapaho National Forests including: James Peak, "Squaw Mountain Lookout," Twin Sisters Lookout, and Arapaho Peak. The record and photograph were recorded for "pre suppression guard planning" or wildfire mitigation.

Subject Geographic#5: North Arapaho Peak (Grand, Colorado, United States, North America) (summit)
